description: Modify the MedicationReminderAlert component to replace the simple dismiss behavior with a pop-up section containing Yes/No radio buttons asking if the user took the medicine. After confirming their answer, close the alert and save the medicine intake to history via the existing server action.
steps: In app/components/MedicationReminderAlert.tsx, add a new server action import: import { recordMedicationIntakeFromAlert } from '@/lib/actions'. This action will be created in the next step.

Create a new server action 'recordMedicationIntakeFromAlert' in lib/actions.ts that accepts a medication ID (number) and a boolean (taken: yes/no). If taken=true, it marks the medication as taken today (updates last_taken_date) and creates a MedicationLog entry using createMedicationLog. If taken=false, it does nothing (or optionally logs a skip). The action must call requireSession(), get userId, call getUserToday(userId) for the date, then call updateMedication and createMedicationLog. Use revalidatePath('/') at the end.

In MedicationReminderAlert.tsx, update the ActiveAlert interface to include a medicationId: number field (the actual database id), so the component can call the server action with it. Update the alert creation code to include the medication's id from the medications prop.

In MedicationReminderAlert.tsx, add local state to track which alert is being interacted with (e.g. activePopupAlertId: string | null) and the user's radio selection (selectedAnswer: 'yes' | 'no' | null).

Replace the current simple dismiss button on each alert card with a pop-up/expanded section UI: When the alert shows, display a question 'Did you take this medicine?' with two radio buttons labeled 'Yes' and 'No', plus a 'Confirm' button. Use a fieldset with legend for accessibility. Style with responsive Tailwind CSS classes and semantic HTML. The pop-up section should appear inline within the alert card (expanding it) rather than as a separate modal.

When the user clicks 'Confirm' after selecting Yes: call the server action recordMedicationIntakeFromAlert(medicationId, true) using useTransition for pending state, then dismiss the alert. When the user clicks Confirm after selecting No: call the server action recordMedicationIntakeFromAlert(medicationId, false), then dismiss the alert.
Disable auto-dismiss (the 60-second timeout) for alerts that have the pop-up active (i.e., alerts that are currently showing the Yes/No question should not auto-dismiss until the user confirms).
Keep the existing browser notification behavior unchanged. The pop-up only enhances the in-app alert card.

Ensure the component still renders with aria-live='polite', uses semantic HTML (fieldset, legend, label elements for the radio group), and uses responsive Tailwind classes per the steering file requirements.
acceptance_criteria: When an alert fires, the alert card shows a question asking if the user took the medicine with Yes/No radio buttons and a Confirm button
Selecting Yes and clicking Confirm calls the server action which updates last_taken_date and creates a MedicationLog entry, then dismisses the alert
Selecting No and clicking Confirm dismisses the alert without marking medication as taken
The Confirm button is disabled until a radio option is selected
The component uses semantic HTML (fieldset, legend, label) for the radio group
All styling uses responsive Tailwind CSS utility classes
bun run build passes without errors
bun run lint passes without errors
verification: Run 'bun run build' and confirm it succeeds without TypeScript or compilation errors
Run 'bun run lint' and confirm no lint errors
